463	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — away-localisation comparison maps
464	
465	The concrete `δ` (coface) and `c` (prepend) maps that the dependent combinatorial
466	core `CombinatorialCech.Dependent.depDiff_exact` consumes are, for the standard
467	affine cover, the canonical comparison maps between away-localisations
468	`M_a → M_b` available whenever `a ∣ b` — so that `a`, a divisor of the invertible
469	`b`, is itself invertible in `M_b`.  This section builds that comparison map and
470	its functoriality from `IsLocalizedModule` abstractly, independent of the sheaf
471	identification (L1), so it can be assembled into the localised {\v C}ech complex.
472	
473	Everything here is `R`-module algebra; no sheaf theory enters. The maps are
474	characterised by uniqueness of localisation lifts (`IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique`),
475	which is what makes the composition/identity laws — the algebraic heart of the
476	`hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm` compatibilities — provable. -/
477	
478	namespace AwayComparison
479	
480	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
481	
482	/-- The clean hypothesis under which a canonical comparison `M_a → Mb` exists: the
483	element `a` acts invertibly on the target localisation `Mb`. Because the scalar
484	action is `algebraMap` into the (central) endomorphism ring, this is closed under
485	multiplication and holds for any divisor of an already-invertible element — exactly
486	the two ways `δ` (divisibility) and `c` (a product of invertible factors) arise. -/
487	def Inverts (a : R) (Mb : Type u) [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb] : Prop :=
488	  IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) a)
489	
490	/-- A divisor `a` of `b` acts invertibly on any localisation `M_b` of `M` away
491	from `b`: since `b` is a unit there and `b = a · c` with the scalar actions
492	commuting (both lie in the image of the central `algebraMap`), `a` is a unit too. -/
493	lemma Inverts.of_dvd {a b : R} (hab : a ∣ b)
494	    {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb] (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb)
495	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb] :
496	    Inverts a Mb := by
497	  obtain ⟨c, rfl⟩ := hab
498	  have hb : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) (a * c)) :=
499	    IsLocalizedModule.map_units fb ⟨a * c, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
500	  rw [map_mul] at hb
501	  have hcomm : Commute (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) a) (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) c) :=
502	    (Commute.all a c).map _
503	  exact (hcomm.isUnit_mul_iff.mp hb).1
504	
505	/-- `Inverts` is closed under multiplication: a product of invertibly-acting
506	elements acts invertibly. This supplies the `c` (prepend) comparison, whose source
507	localising element `s_r · s_{cons r σ}` factors into divisors of the target. -/
508	lemma Inverts.mul {a a' : R} {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
509	    (ha : Inverts a Mb) (ha' : Inverts a' Mb) : Inverts (a * a') Mb := by
510	  rw [Inverts, map_mul]
511	  exact IsUnit.mul ha ha'
512	
513	/-- The power version of the `Inverts` hypothesis, as the `IsLocalizedModule.lift`
514	universal property consumes it (`∀ x ∈ powers a`). -/
515	lemma Inverts.isUnit_powers {a : R} {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
516	    (ha : Inverts a Mb) (x : Submonoid.powers a) :
517	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) (x : R)) := by
518	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
519	  rw [← hn, map_pow]
520	  exact ha.pow n
521	
522	/-- The canonical comparison map `M_a → Mb` between localisations of `M`, defined
523	whenever `a` acts invertibly on `Mb` (`Inverts a Mb`). It is the unique `R`-linear
524	map `M_a → Mb` commuting with the localisation structure maps (`comparison_unique`);
525	this universal characterisation is what makes the functoriality laws below provable. -/
526	noncomputable def comparison {a : R}
527	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
528	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
529	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
530	    Ma →ₗ[R] Mb :=
531	  IsLocalizedModule.lift (Submonoid.powers a) fa fb hb.isUnit_powers
532	
533	@[simp] lemma comparison_apply {a : R}
534	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
535	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
536	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) (x : M) :
537	    comparison fa fb hb (fa x) = fb x :=
538	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply _ _ _ _ x
539	
540	lemma comparison_comp_structure {a : R}
541	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
542	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
543	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
544	    comparison fa fb hb ∘ₗ fa = fb :=
545	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_comp _ _ _ _
546	
547	/-- Uniqueness: any `R`-linear `l : M_a → Mb` commuting with the structure maps
548	equals the comparison map. -/
549	lemma comparison_unique {a : R}
550	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
551	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
552	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb)
553	    (l : Ma →ₗ[R] Mb) (hl : l ∘ₗ fa = fb) :
554	    comparison fa fb hb = l :=
555	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique _ _ _ _ l hl
556	
557	/-- **Identity law.** The comparison of a localisation with itself is the identity. -/
558	@[simp] lemma comparison_self {a : R}
559	    {Ma : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma]

1636	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
1637	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
1638	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
1639	  change Function.Exact
1640	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
1641	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)))
1642	  rw [hf, hg]
1643	  exact h
1644	
1645	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum. -/
1646	private lemma ab_hom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
1647	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
1648	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
1649	  classical
1650	  induction s using Finset.induction with
1651	  | empty => simp
1652	  | insert a s ha ih =>
1653	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
1654	
1655	/-- The `i`-th section {\v C}ech face restriction at a multi-index `σ` of the
1656	cosimplicial section object: the presheaf restriction of `F` from the deleted-face
1657	intersection `⨅ₗ U (σ (δ i l))` to the full intersection `⨅ₖ U (σ k)`.  Factored as a
1658	named `def` so the cosimplicial differential (`sectionCech_objD_apply`) refers to it
1659	by name rather than re-elaborating the `homOfLE (le_iInf …)` term. -/
1660	noncomputable def sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1661	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1662	    F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1663	      ⟶ F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) :=
1664	  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf
1665	    (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op
1666	
1667	/-- **(c2, abstract) Coface match**: the underlying group action of the section
1668	{\v C}ech coface differential `objD q`, read through the product equivalence, is the
1669	alternating sum of the presheaf restriction maps applied to the deleted-face
1670	coordinates — exactly the shape of `SectionCechModule.dDiff`.  This is purely about
1671	the cosimplicial structure of `sectionCechCosimplicial`; no sheaf identification yet. -/
1672	lemma sectionCech_objD_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1673	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1674	    (t : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk q)))
1675	    (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) :
1676	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F (q + 1)
1677	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1678	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t) σ
1679	      = ∑ i : Fin (q + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1680	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
1681	            (sectionCechProductEquiv U F q t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
1682	  classical
1683	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
1684	      show ConcreteCategory.hom
1685	            (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ)
1686	            (ConcreteCategory.hom
1687	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t)
1688	          = ConcreteCategory.hom
1689	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q
1690	                ≫ Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) t
1691	          from rfl,
1692	      AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Preadditive.sum_comp, ab_hom_finsetSum_apply]
1693	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1694	  rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1695	  simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_zsmul, AddMonoidHom.smul_apply]
1696	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]
1697	  congr 1
1698	  have hmap : (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).map (SimplexCategory.δ i) ≫ Pi.π _ σ
1699	      = Pi.π _ (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫ sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i :=
1700	    Pi.lift_π _ σ
1701	  rw [CosimplicialObject.δ, hmap]
1702	  rfl
1703	
1704	end SectionCechBridge
1705	
1706	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — tilde-bridge and affine vanishing (L1 steps A, B, d)
1707	
1708	The final stretch of the L1 categorical→module bridge for the **tilde sheaf**
1709	`F = ~M`.  The previous section moved the abstract `Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex
1710	to the shape of an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions
1711	(`sectionCech_objD_apply`); here we identify, degreewise, those section groups with the
1712	away-localisation coefficients `dCoeff` and the restrictions with the localisation
1713	cofaces `dCoface`, via the per-σ comparison `φ_σ` (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  Transporting
1714	the already-proven module exactness `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the resulting
1715	degreewise additive isomorphism (`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`) discharges
1716	the underlying-group exactness, which `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` turns
1717	into positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech complex
1718	(`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`). -/
1719	
1720	section SectionCechTilde
1721	
1722	open AlgebraicTopology Scheme.Modules SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison
1723	
1724	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
1725	
1726	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1727	-- raised: the `IsLocalizedModule.ext` reduction over the heavy `modulesSpecToSheaf` section
1728	-- types is defeq-intensive (the accessor-2 `~M` sections do not reduce cheaply).
1729	/-- **Linear-level per-coface naturality** (`R`-linear).  The comparison `φ_σ`
1730	(written here as the raw `IsLocalizedModule.iso ▸ symm`, definitionally `phiL` below)
1731	intertwines the (accessor-2) {\v C}ech face restriction with the away-localisation coface
1732	`dCoface`.  Proved by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`: both composites send the localisation unit
1733	`toOpen M (D s_{σ∘dᵢ})` of `M` to the localisation unit `mkLinearMap (powers s_σ)`, by
1734	`tilde.toOpen_res` (restriction commutes with `toOpen`), `iso_symm_comp`, and
1735	`comparison_apply` (the defining property of `dCoface`).  Stated and proved *before* the
1736	`phiL` abbreviation so its `IsLocalizedModule.ext` elaboration is not slowed by
1737	fold/unfold attempts against `phiL` — keeping it within the heartbeat budget. -/
1738	private lemma phiL_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1739	    haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1740	    (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1741	        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
1742	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1743	          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1744	            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
1745	      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ
1746	        (haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1747	         (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1748	          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k,
1749	            PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap) := by

/-! ### The non-augmented core comparison `coreIso` (blueprint `lem:coreIso_*`).

The three lemmas below build, degreewise object iso + differential match, the non-augmented
core iso `(G_V ∘ Ψ) Č•(𝒰, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰', F)` that `cechSection_complex_iso` glues to the
augmentation data via `augmentCochainIso`. -/

/-- **Open-meet identity** (`lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib`): intersecting the cover-meet
`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ = ⨅ₖ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)` with an open `V` distributes factorwise over the
nonempty `Fin (p+1)`-indexed meet. -/
lemma coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ}
    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V) := by
  rw [coverInterOpen]
  exact iInf_inf

/-- **Degreewise object iso of the non-augmented core comparison** (`lem:coreIso_obj_iso`).
The degree-`p` term of the evaluated non-augmented backbone complex
`Γ(V, Ψ(pushPullObj F Y_p))` is isomorphic, as an abelian group, to the degree-`p` term
`∏_σ Γ(⨅ₖ(coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V), F)` of the concrete restricted section Čech complex.
This is `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4) post-composed with the factorwise reindexing along
`coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf`. -/
noncomputable def coreIso_objIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
          (pushPullObj F
            ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))))).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op V) ≅
      (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X p :=
  pushPull_eval_prod_iso 𝒰 F p V ≪≫
    Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
      eqToIso (congrArg
        (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))
        (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 σ V)))

/-- **The core comparison intertwines the Čech differentials** (`lem:coreIso_comm`).  Under the
degreewise object isos `coreIso_objIso`, the alternating-coface differential of the evaluated
backbone complex `(G_V ∘ Ψ) Č•(𝒰, F)` matches the alternating-coface differential of the
concrete restricted section complex `Č•(𝒰', F)`.  The proof expands both differentials as
alternating sums of cofaces (`AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD`), pushes the additive functors
`Ψ`, `G_V` through the sum, and matches the per-coface squares (the residual `coreIso` content). -/
lemma coreIso_comm (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i j : ℕ) (hij : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).Rel i j) :
    (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F i V).hom ≫ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).d i j =
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
            (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj
              (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
          (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
              PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapHomologicalComplex
            (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F)) |>.d i j ≫
        (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F j V).hom := by
  obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
  haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).Additive :=
    inferInstance
  rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d]
  sorry

Γ(U_i ∩ V, F)`) transported across the degree-`0` object iso `coreIso_objIso`.  This is the

noncomputable def sectionCechAugV (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V) ⟶
      (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X 0 :=
  (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)) ≫
    (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom

Transported from the backbone identity `cechAugmentation_comp_d` through `coreIso_comm`. -/
